mutation:
composite: goal changed from a decision flood-plan to an optimization plan (maximize a
  weighted dry-zone objective under a staged, cumulative, irreversible wall budget), and input
  generalized from a single flood event to a K-stage rising-threshold schedule over an elevation
  grid with a competing secondary (forbidden) zone -- an open, no-known-optimum staged
  barrier-placement problem.
theme: Wall off zones as a flood level rises in stages
title: Rising Water, Staged Barriers
strategies: trivial: build no walls at all (baseline, ratio ~0.1)

greedy: reactive perimeter defense -- each stage, wall the open edges of any dry cell about to flood that sits within a small grid-distance of the protected zone, closest first, ignoring the forbidden zone entirely; cannot distinguish a real corridor from a nearby dead-end notch and burns budget on decoys

strong: per-zone exact min-edge-cut via max-flow on the graph of ever-eligible cells, ignoring any cell/path that carries no source-to-zone flow (dead ends vanish automatically); brute-force selection of the best-value subset of zones (protected zones + the forbidden zone) that is schedulable within the staged cumulative budget, respecting each cut edge's elevation deadline

open: joint global scheduling + selection across many zones with interacting deadlines and a single shared cumulative budget has no known polynomial-optimal algorithm at scale (a budgeted, deadline-constrained cut-selection problem)
